target_output.thinking_summary: 'IoT telemetry ingestion pipeline requires high-throughput architecture balancing partner OEM access security with internal analytics performance.' Decision favors Flink windowed aggregation over Spark Structured Streaming due to sub-second latency targets.
target_output.title: 'Agritech Telemetry Streaming Architecture'
target_output.executive_summary: 'High-throughput IoT data pipeline ingesting soil and crop telemetry from partner devices using EMQX MQTT broker into Kafka topics processed by Flink jobs and persisted in TimescaleDB. Provides aggregated insights to farm dashboards through secure internal APIs.'
target_output.architecture_overview: 'Three-layer design with EMQX broker cluster receiving mTLS connections from field sensors. Kafka MSK handles topic partitioning per farm region. Flink job operators compute rolling averages and anomaly detection within 1-min windows. Results published to TimescaleDB with Grafana overlays available via internal API gateway.' All networking secured via private VPC peering between broker nodes and processing cluster.
target_output.technology_stack: 'EMQX v5 broker cluster on EKS nodes; Kafka MSK 3-broker setup (64 partitions); Apache Flink 1.17 deployment via Helm charts; TimescaleDB 2.13 PostgreSQL extension; S3 Iceberg tables for historical archives; Terraform-managed infrastructure and CI/CD using Argo Workflows.' Each component monitored through Prometheus exporters.
target_output.functional_feature_map: 'Device registration microservice manages sensor metadata schema (farm_id, device_id, coordinates). Stream ingestion handles MQTT topic mapping per farm region. Flink aggregation computes soil moisture averages and nutrient variance. Dashboard API exposes region-level summaries to partner analytics frontend via REST endpoints.' Data lineage documented using DataHub metadata registry integration.
target_output.system_components: 'EMQX MQTT Broker Cluster (multi-AZ) handling up to 50K concurrent devices), Kafka MSK Cluster (persistent streams), Flink Job Manager + Task Managers (aggregation), TimescaleDB primary replica pair, S3 bucket for archival Iceberg tables, API Gateway exposing REST endpoints for dashboard consumption.' Each component isolated in network namespaces within VPC subnets.
target_output.workflows: 'Sensor Data Ingestion': Device publishes readings to topic 'farm/{id}/soil', broker authenticates via client certificate, message enters Kafka partition keyed by farm_id. 'Aggregation Job': Flink consumes batches every minute, computes average moisture & nutrient index, writes result to TimescaleDB table 'farm_metrics'. 'Analytics Export': Nightly batch job extracts region-level metrics into CSV uploaded to S3 for external data science partners.' Distinct event paths ensure resilience under network jitter scenarios.
target_output.data_model: 'devices(device_id UUID PK, farm_id FK, lat FLOAT, lon FLOAT, model VARCHAR). readings(id UUID PK, device_id FK, timestamp TIMESTAMPTZ, soil_moisture FLOAT, temperature FLOAT, nutrient_ppm FLOAT). farm_metrics(farm_id UUID PK, window_start TIMESTAMPTZ, avg_moisture FLOAT, avg_temp FLOAT, avg_nutrient FLOAT). anomalies(id UUID PK, farm_id FK, metric_type ENUM moisture/temp/nutrient). All stored using Timescale hypertables with compression policies after 14 days.' Data integrity validated via Flink checkpointing.
target_output.api_design: 'POST /v1/devices/register', 'GET /v1/farms/{id}/metrics', 'GET /v1/anomalies?farm_id={uuid}', 'POST /v1/export/start'. Authentication handled by internal IAM tokens validated at API Gateway layer.' Rate limit per partner org set at 1000 requests/minute. Versioned endpoints maintain backward compatibility with older dashboard clients.' Response format JSON schema aligned to internal DataHub models.
target_output.security_and_compliance: 'All broker connections require mTLS certificates issued by internal CA rotated every 90 days. IAM policies restrict Flink job write access to TimescaleDB schema only. Kafka ACL controls producer/consumer groups per vendor ID. Audit logs persisted in S3 immutable bucket daily rotation. Compliance ensures no cross-farm data leakage through partition misconfiguration checks.' WAF rules block unauthorized partner IPs outside registered list.
target_output.deployment_and_operations: 'CI/CD via Argo Workflows triggers Helm upgrades of Flink jobs after successful unit validation. Terraform modules provision EMQX clusters across two AZs with autoscaling node groups. Kafka brokers monitored for lag thresholds triggering horizontal scaling actions. Backup tasks run nightly exporting TimescaleDB snapshots to S3 Glacier Deep Archive.' Blue-green deployments allow seamless upgrade without message loss using consumer offset migration script.' Metrics collected continuously by Prometheus agent pods aggregated into Grafana dashboards per farm region view.
target_output.observability: 'Prometheus collects Kafka lag metrics (topic_partition_lag), Flink operator throughput (records/sec), EMQX connection count per node. Loki centralized logging captures broker disconnect events tagged by device_id. Grafana panels visualize time-series trends including soil moisture fluctuations across regions.' Alerts configured in Alertmanager for sustained lag >30s or anomaly detection job failures beyond threshold count of 10/hour.
target_output.cost_and_scaling: 'Expected monthly spend $4.7K across EKS compute & MSK storage under normal load of 30K devices streaming every minute.' Scaling managed automatically based on CPU utilization of Flink TaskManagers (~60%). Reserved instance commitment reduces cost ~25%. Archival tier costs <$40/month due to Iceberg compaction scheduling weekly.' Future expansion to additional regions planned within same cluster footprint without major redesign.'
target_output.phased_implementation: 'Phase 1 (8 weeks): Deploy EMQX cluster and ingest from pilot farms (500 devices). Phase 2 (10 weeks): Scale Kafka partitions & implement anomaly detection operator in Flink jobs. Phase 3 (12 weeks): Add dashboard API export endpoints & integrate DataHub lineage tracking. Phase 4 (continuous): Optimize compression and retention policies based on usage metrics.' Each phase includes load validation tests simulating regional device bursts up to projected seasonal peak volumes.'
target_output.development_guidelines: 'All infrastructure defined in Terraform modules version-controlled in GitLab repo; PRs require peer review from DevOps lead.' Developers must write integration tests verifying Flink window correctness using embedded Kafka test harnesses.' Code style enforced via Google Java format plugin on Flink jobs repository.' Documentation stored in Confluence project space with automatic diagram generation from Helm charts using Diagrams-as-Code toolset.'
target_output.risks_and_tradeoffs: 'Risk of high network jitter causing delayed readings aggregation mitigated via buffer watermark adjustments in Flink job config.' Tradeoff between compression ratio vs query latency handled by setting chunk interval at 24h instead of default 7d reducing retrieval time by ~30%.' Risk of vendor misconfigured certificates mitigated by automated mTLS provisioning workflow integrated with Partner Portal registration flow.'
target_output.open_questions_resolved: 'Decided against Spark Structured Streaming due to startup latency overhead compared to long-running Flink tasks; selected TimescaleDB over InfluxDB because of SQL compatibility needed by downstream analysts; clarified AWS MSK cluster retention policy at 72h ensuring recovery window adequate for delayed farm uploads.'

metadata.notes: 'High-scale agritech IoT streaming design demonstrating robust domain specificity.'
